title: The Summer Movie Mailbag
format: The last Sean-and-Amanda episode of the summer is a listener mailbag that mostly skips the summer that just happened. It wanders through July 4th traditions, the movies that taught them to swear, summertime-sadness double bills, cocktail pairings, a fall-slate traffic jam, and a long detour explaining Fortnite.

segments.heading: Taking stock of the summer

films.title: Sinners
films.note: The only Best Picture contender they think is currently in play.

films.title: Materialists
films.note: The Celine Song movie people went to see and got mad about, Sean's favorite discourse of the year.

films.title: One Battle After Another
films.note: The PTA movie hanging over the whole fall; at least three episodes promised.

films.title: Michael Clayton
films.note: A 25-for-25 screening Sean is presenting at the Aero while Amanda's in Venice.

segments.heading: Your summer movie MVP
segments.note: Tyler — catch: can't be a movie

films.title: Zootopia
films.note: Celine Song's desert-island pick, to her own bemusement.

films.title: Weapons
films.note: Sean hasn't seen it at record time; sold by old-school analog marketing.

films.title: Mission: Impossible - The Final Reckoning
films.note: Didn't love it, but the biplanes were 20 minutes of cinema.

films.title: Smurfs
films.note: The only Smurfs content anyone absorbed: Rihanna's kids in Baby Dior at the premiere.

segments.heading: 4th of July viewing traditions

films.title: Independence Day
films.note: The obvious answer.

films.title: Jaws

films.title: An American Tail: Fievel Goes West
Blow Out

films.title: Nashville
films.note: Altman will come up again in the near future.

films.note: Amanda's summer answer: Americans abroad in Keds betraying each other.

films.note: Underdiscussed Nancy Meyers; they meet at camp.

films.title: Kicking and Screaming
films.note: Baumbach's back-to-school movie, an all-timer for Sean.

segments.heading: Formative in your swearing education

films.title: Casino
films.note: Once held the record for F-bombs; they say it like poetry.

films.title: Four Weddings and a Funeral
films.note: Amanda at 10, when the Brits treated cursing as poetry.

films.title: Clueless
films.note: No real curses, but hugely formative for how to talk.

segments.heading: Summertime sadness films

films.title: The Green Ray

films.title: Adventureland
films.note: Archetypal, and it nails summer as a time of longing.

films.title: Crooklyn
films.note: Spike Lee's memory movie, based on his sister's summer down south.

films.title: La Collectionneuse
films.note: Another Rohmer summer movie Sean recommends.

films.title: Unrelated
films.note: Early Joanna Hogg vacation movie.

films.title: Melancholia
films.note: Wedding season, short sleeves on the golf course: summer-coded.

films.title: A Brighter Summer Day
films.note: Edward Yang; summer in the title if not literally a summer movie.

segments.heading: Adult, steamy, sun-soaked summer movies

films.title: Body Heat
films.note: William Hurt sweating through cotton poplin in Florida.

films.title: The Thomas Crown Affair
films.note: Sex on a marble staircase; a remake being remade again with Michael B. Jordan.

films.title: The Thomas Crown Affair
films.note: The Steve McQueen original.

films.title: I Am Love
films.note: Slept-on Guadagnino; sex scenes in a field with bees.

films.title: Bones and All
films.note: Sean's overlap pick for Mackenzie and Stephanie: sexy and sweaty.

films.title: Sense and Sensibility

segments.heading: Habits shaped by the people in your life

films.title: Sinners
films.note: Brent's girlfriend broke her no-horror rule on Amanda's word and now loves it.

films.title: What's Your Number?
films.note: Amanda met her husband outside a screening of it.

films.title: Top Gun: Maverick

films.title: Diner

segments.heading: Where's the Daniel Day-Lewis hype?

films.note: Bill the Butcher, he rocks.

films.title: Nanou
films.note: One of the obscure mid-'80s DDL films Sean wants to fill in.

films.title: Stars and Bars
films.note: Another obscure DDL film on his catch-up list.
